data.content:
The build confirms the file is generated but not compiled. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but never adds it to the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile>` item for the generated file inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates a `.cs` file, but it doesn't add it to the `Compile` item group. The file is generated but MSBuild doesn't know to include it in compilation.

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using `<ItemGroup>` with a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` after the file is written.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

  </Target>", "new_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>"}

  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target generated `GeneratedInfo.cs` into the intermediate output directory but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild wrote the file to disk, but the C# compiler had no knowledge of it.

**Fix:** Added a `<Compile Include="...">` item inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```
